title: MASLOW PROJECT - 211info
text: GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER MASLOW PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Drop in hours: Monday-Friday 1pm-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 841-1974 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for more information. Chat feature is available on website. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Josephine County Services: Bedding/Linen * Homeless Families Bedding/Linen * Homeless Youth Camping Gear * Homeless Families Camping Gear * Homeless Youth Case/Care Management * Homeless Families Case/Care Management * Homeless Youth Certificates/Forms Assistance * Homeless Families Certificates/Forms Assistance * Homeless Youth Diapers * Homeless Families Diapers * Homeless Youth Food Pantries * Homeless Families Food Pantries * Homeless Youth Formula/Baby Food * Homeless Families Formula/Baby Food * Homeless Youth General Clothing Provision * Homeless Families General Clothing Provision * Homeless Youth Homeless Drop In Centers * At Risk Families Homeless Drop In Centers * At Risk Youth Homelessness Advocacy Groups * At Risk Families Homelessness Advocacy Groups * At Risk Youth Laundry Facilities * Homeless Families Laundry Facilities * Homeless Youth Personal/Grooming Supplies * Homeless Families Personal/Grooming Supplies * Homeless Youth Pet Food * Homeless Families Pet Food * Homeless Youth Public Internet Access Sites * Homeless Families Public Internet Access Sites * Homeless Youth Runaway/Homeless Youth Counseling Specialized Information and Referral * Homeless Families Specialized Information and Referral * Homeless Youth Temporary Mailing Address * Homeless Families Temporary Mailing Address * Homeless Youth If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
